channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Harris and Trump are tested by the Mideast, Helene and the port strike in the campaign's final weeks
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A trio of new trials — a devastating hurricane, expanding conflict in the Mideast and a dockworkers strike that threatens the U.S.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, ZEKE MILLER and THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Several questions about Walz's record not asked about during vice presidential debate
item.description: Several controversial moments from Gov. Tim Walz's past were not mentioned during the first and only vice presidential debate on Tuesday night in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Walz faces backlash after defending Obama-era mandate repealed by Trump: 'Massive tax penalty'
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz defended the merits of an individual mandate for healthcare after being pressed by Sen. JD Vance during the vice presidential debate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Conservative social media reactions pour in declaring winner of VP debate: 'This was a massacre'
item.description: Social media reactions from conservatives came pouring in on social media following the first and only vice presidential debate on Tuesday night in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Jersey Democrat proposes bill to create travel advisories to inform pregnant women of state abortion laws
item.description: A New Jersey Democrat proposed a bill to create travel advisories to notify women of restrictive abortion laws in other states they may be visiting.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: From Walz’s hometown to an American Legion, Minnesotans react to governor in VP debate
item.description: Supporters of the Republican and Democratic presidential tickets gathered Tuesday evening for debate watch parties around Minnesota for the likely only debate between Sen. J.D. Vance of Ohio and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik and Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: In VP debate, Walz acknowledges he wasn’t in China during Tiananmen uprising
item.description: APM Reports, the investigations and documentaries unit of MPR News, first reported that Walz’s claim during a 2014 congressional hearing on the 25th anniversary of the massacre didn’t square up with media accounts in 1989.
item.dc:creator: Tom Scheck, Curtis Gilbert, Caspar von Au, and Forest Hunt, MPR News

item.title: Vance and Walz keep it civil in a policy-heavy discussion: VP debate takeaways
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice presidential hopefuls Tim Walz and JD Vance focused their criticism on the top of the ticket on Tuesday as they engaged in a policy-heavy discussion that may be the last debate of the 2024 presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, ZEKE MILLER and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walz’s account of son’s brush with gun violence offers poignant moment in VP debate
item.description: “Look, I’ve got a 17-year-old and he witnessed a shooting at a community center playing volleyball,” Walz said during the debate, referencing his son Gus. “Those things don’t leave you.
item.dc:creator: Sam Stroozas, MPR News

item.title: Walz and Vance go in depth on policy while attacking each other’s running mates in VP debate
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — In a debate that evoked a calmer era in American politics, Tim Walz and JD Vance on Tuesday went after each other's running mates and sought to shore up their campaigns' vulnerabilities at a time of renewed fears of a regional war in the Middle East and sadness over devastation from
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, CHRIS MEGERIAN, WILL WEISSSERT and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance, Walz address school shootings
item.description: Walz said the solution is more research into gun violence. Vance said we have to increase security in our schools.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Walz, Vance double down on dueling abortion views
item.description: November 2024 will mark the first U.S. presidential election since the Supreme Court overturned its decision in Roe v. Wade.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Fact-check fight: CBS News cuts candidates' mics after JD Vance objects to a moderator's correction
item.description: CBS News briefly muted the debate microphones of the two vice presidential candidates after Republican JD Vance objected to a fact-check made by Margaret Brennan, one of the network's moderators.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Voters react to Gov. Tim Walz claiming abortion is a 'basic human right'
item.description: Fox News Digital's dial group made up of independent, Democratic and Republican voters differed on Democratic Gov. Tim Walz's comments on abortion in Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: After pressing an Israel-Hezbollah cease-fire, the Biden administration shifts its message
item.description: The Biden administration says there's a significant difference between Israeli actions that have expanded its war against the Iranian-backed militant groups Hamas and Hezbollah and Iran’s retaliatory missile attack against Israel.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee And Lolita C. Baldor, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Republicans are more likely than Democrats to see Israel as a US ally: AP-NORC poll
item.description: A new Pearson Institute/AP-NORC survey finds Republicans are more likely to see Israel as an ally that shares the U.S.'s interests and values, while Democrats are more likely to see Israel as a partner that doesn’t share U.S. interests and values.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Thomson-Deveaux, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump won't participate in interview for '60 Minutes' election special
item.description: Trump's campaign spokesman said, “Fake news," adding that there were discussions but nothing was ever locked in.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Fox News Politics: VP Debate Night
item.description: The latest updates from the 2024 campaign trail, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NY race for US Senate: Sapraicone on the border, abortion, gun control
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – With a Senate seat up for grabs, Republican Mike Sapraicone joined PIX11 News on Tuesday night to discuss gun control, abortion, crime, and other key issues. The retired NYPD detective will take on incumbent Democratic Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand in the Nov. 5 general election.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Trump speaks in Wisconsin ahead of VP debate
item.description: The former president criticized Kamala Harris and Joe Biden for their handling of the conflict in the Middle East and the border.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Eric Adams appoints new chief counsel amid string of resignations
item.description: Prosecutors unsealed an indictment last week alleging Adams took bribes and illegal campaign contributions.
item.dc:creator: Armando Cataldi, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans eager to keep House control are open to Trump’s SALT reversal
item.description: Republicans are open to raising the state and local tax (SALT) deduction cap that the party championed and put in place under former President Trump seven years ago as they aim to win total control of government in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: NATO's new chief makes Ukraine support a top priority and says he will work with any US president
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The new head of NATO vowed on Tuesday to help shore up Western support for war-ravaged Ukraine and expressed confidence that he can work with whoever is elected president of the United States, the alliance's most powerful member, in November.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Photos: As Jimmy Carter celebrates his 100th birthday, a look through the decades
item.description: Former President Jimmy Carter is turning 100. Here are images from notable events or parts of his life, both during his presidency and after.
item.dc:creator: James Doubek, MPR News

item.title: How young Republicans and Democrats are whipping up votes in key districts
item.description: As the presidential race ramps up in Georgia, one vital voting demographic is mobilizing and hoping to impact the race: young people.
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly, Erika Ryan, Kira Wakeam, and Courtney Dorning, MPR News

item.title: Rick Scott calls for Senate to reconvene to consider Helene disaster aid
item.description: Sen. Rick Scott (R-Fla.) on Monday called for the Senate to “immediately reconvene” once damage assessments are completed so lawmakers can consider an emergency aid package in response to the devastation left by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Trump suspect pleads not guilty in attempted assassination
item.description: The man accused of attempting to assassinate former President Trump at one of his Florida golf courses earlier this month has pleaded not guilty to five federal charges, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: PIX11 News to host NY-18 debate with candidates Pat Ryan and Alison Esposito
item.description: September 30, 2024, New York, NY – PIX11 News today announced it will host a live debate for New York’s 18th Congressional District. Democratic incumbent Congressman Pat Ryan, a retired military intelligence officer in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: PIX 11

item.title: Harris trolls Trump at Vegas rally and LA fundraiser, says her crowds are 'pretty big'
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris poked at Donald Trump over crowd sizes, his refusal to debate again and his privileged background on Sunday as she hauled in campaign cash in California and held a raucous rally at the same Nevada venue where the Republican nominee had appeared just two
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: California Gov. Newsom vetoes AI safety bill that divided Silicon Valley
item.description: The measure, known as SB 1047, was one of the nation’s most far-reaching regulations on the booming AI industry. It would have held AI companies legally liable for harms caused by AI and enabled a "kill switch" if systems went rogue.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Tim Walz’s experience in China could help him as veep, but he barely mentions it as a candidate
item.description: When Gov. Tim Walz first ran for Congress in 2006, his campaign proudly touted his extensive experience in China. In fact, earlier in his political career, he occasionally exaggerated it. But now that he’s running for vice president, Walz barely mentions China.
item.dc:creator: Forest Hunt, Caspar von Au, and Curtis Gilbert, MPR News

item.title: Exploring the Eric Adams indictment for bribery, wire fraud, and solicitating contributions from Turkish nationals
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams pleaded not guilty in a federal bribery case after being indicted on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here are the rules for the Vance-Walz debate
item.description: The debate, which will kick off at 9 p.m. EDT on Tuesday from the network’s New York office, will be moderated by CBS anchor Norah O’Donnell and "Face the Nation" host Margaret Brennan.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump tops Harris by 6 points in Arizona: Survey
item.description: The new USA TODAY/Suffolk University survey found Trump leads the vice president, 48 percent to 42 percent, in the Grand Canyon State.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Senate candidates Gillibrand, Sapraicone to face off in PIX11 forum
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Election Day is just a few weeks away. Do you know who you’re voting for in the New York race for U.S. Senate? Republican candidate Mike Sapraicone is battling to unseat Democratic Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Cook, PIX 11

item.title: Prop. 36 faces intense review as North County residents gather for informative session
item.description: Voters have a lot to consider this upcoming election when it comes to Proposition 36, a statewide ballot measure that seeks a tougher approach on crime.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Franco, Fox 5

item.title: Secret Service agent placed on leave after 'misconduct allegation' with Harris staffer
item.description: The U.S. Secret Service recently notified Vice President Kamala Harris’ office about an incident involving one of its agents and a “misconduct allegation,” according to spokespeople for both the USSS and the vice president's office.
item.dc:creator: Monica Alba and Kelly O'Donnell, WFLA, NBC
